description: PLMKR Digital operations knowledge base — load order for knowledge assembly

files.description: Specialist identity and mission (metadata as the routing table for royalties), jurisdiction (owns/does-not-own), philosophy, decision style and biases, communication style, eight-item judgment doctrine (completeness threshold, ISRC reuse tree, claim dispute protocol, error severity, NOT EVALUABLE, anti-fabrication/currency rule, audit triage, delivery-failure triage), hard refusals

DDEX ERN/ID3 metadata standards, required vs optional fields; ISRC anatomy and assignment protocol, reuse rules, UPC/EAN anatomy, common ISRC errors and remediation cost, identifier interoperability (ISRC vs ISWC)

Currency warning; universal delivery requirements (audio/artwork/metadata), platform comparison, delivery vs editorial timeline, rejection cause taxonomy, pre-delivery QC checklist, domain anti-patterns

Content-recognition system architecture and claim types, claim evaluation protocol, dispute decision tree, ISRC-conflict to wrongful-claim chain, TikTok rights layer, Meta rights manager, abuse patterns and responses

Four-pillar governance framework, version control for metadata, metadata debt and remediation sequencing, catalog scale thresholds, standards maintenance, seven-coverage-area rights-hygiene audit, dark-catalog problem, territory-block remediation discipline

Eight-dimension release-quality scorecard, grade anchors, four hard gates (ISRC conflict, delivery rejection, erroneous claim, territory liability), action tiers, composite/calibration discipline, NOT EVALUABLE discipline

Mandatory core and conditional-module matrix, four deliverable templates (Digital Release QC Report, Metadata Error Remediation Plan, Rights Hygiene Audit Report, Content Recognition Claim Evaluation), handoff boundaries, escalation flags, hard refusals

notes.entity_wall: These files are PLMKR-owned originals. Do not reference external sources, rubric identifiers, sibling products, or entity names from any source material.
notes.mock_mode: Deterministic knowledge assembly only — no live LLM/API calls during loading or testing.
